name: Baked Tilapia with Whole Spices
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons peanut or other vegetable oil
1 medium carrot, peeled and cut into 4-inch sticks
8 quarter-size slices ginger (no need to peel)
recipeInstructions:
This recipe works with other whole lean fish, such as small rockfish, farmed striped bass, halibut, or pink salmon. Adjust the cooking time according to the thickness and density of the fish.
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.
2. Rub a baking dish well with oil, add carrots, green onions, ginger, allspice, star anise, peppercorns, and dried chiles. Toss to coat lightly.
3. Arrange in an even layer. Rinse tilapia fillets inside and out, pat dry, season with salt, and place on top of the vegetables.
4. Add wine and 1 tablespoon stock. Cover the tail of the fish loosely with foil and bake for 25-30 minutes, rotating halfway through.
5. Fish is done when a skewer easily enters the thickest part (145-150 degrees F). Transfer to a warm platter.
6. Strain the remaining liquid from the dish into a saucepan, discard spices and vegetables. Add remaining stock, bring to boil, then reduce by third. Adjust seasoning if needed.
7. Swirl in butter if desired and pour over fish.
